sources.notes: Statement and medium constraints n, q <= 200000.
Official editorial and comments used to confirm the chain/Ferrers invariant.
Hard-version O(n+q) invariant solution, also valid for medium.

annotation.summary: Cells of an initially white n by n matrix are turned black online; after each insertion, decide if row/column permutations can avoid the forbidden alternating 2x2 pattern.
annotation.core_idea: A valid matrix is exactly a Ferrers/chain-graph matrix after permuting rows and columns: sorted rows have nested neighborhoods. Maintain the actual row-pair contribution and the canonical Ferrers contribution induced by column heights. The matrix is valid iff these contribution sums are equal.
annotation.complexity: O(n+q) time and O(n) memory per total input.
tricks: represent the matrix as a bipartite chain graph
compare row-degree contribution with canonical column-height contribution

solution_variants.name: degree-contribution-invariant
solution_variants.summary: Maintain sr = sum C(row_black_count,2) and sc = sum over column-height layers C(count_ge_height,2); answer YES iff sr == sc.

tags.evidence: The monotone condition is equivalent to the black-cell bipartite graph being a chain graph with nested row neighborhoods.
tags.description: Bipartite graphs whose neighborhoods on one side are nested; equivalently Ferrers or monotone binary matrices with no induced 2K2.
tags.created_reason: The existing bipartite-graph tag is too broad for Ferrers/chain-matrix characterizations used in matrix and graph problems.

tags.evidence: The online solution works by updating two contribution sums after each inserted black cell.
The invariant acts as a compact fingerprint of row-pair contributions for the actual and canonical matrices.
The characterization relies on rows and columns being sortable into a monotone Ferrers shape.
